title: PreVillage: public-service knowledge before privilege
timing_notes: B01-B07 are on the original narration clock. B08 inserts the Jiri mayor quote; B09-B20 are retimed to the editorial output clock after the mayor insert and the small WhatsApp example-phrase replacement. Only the spoken example phrase is removed from founder VO; the rest of the narration remains intact.
codex_fixes: Added global_assets so narration audio and Chirp2 timing files are accounted for outside visible beat assets.
Bound montage/source assets to visible layers instead of leaving them only in beat-level assets.
Made the B04 process reference image explicitly visible as faint texture instead of a non-visible reference.
Selected B14 interview source window 0:50-1:14 for practical-source proof.
Reduced late architecture repetition by using the architecture graphic primarily as a system reveal, not every closing beat.
Shifted B09 from confusing wordplay toward already-learned UX, with the hen only as a sub-second easter egg.
Added clearer ASR, Gemma correction, retrieval, TTS, and kiosk proof across B17-B18.

Applied delivery-v2 fixes: removed unsupported PDF placeholder from B02, added selected Jiri source audio, added scratch spoken close, removed beat fade-through-black, and promoted WhatsApp/RAG/Pi/voice proof beats to custom layouts.
Replaced the scratch spoken close with an eleven-person question grid using Chirp2 word-level timestamps.

typography.latin_display: Inter Black or equivalent local sans
typography.nepali: Noto Sans Devanagari / system Devanagari fallback
typography.caption: large, high contrast, no decorative letter spacing

audio_design.narration_priority: Narration is the lead instrument. Background score must stay quiet enough that every word remains intelligible.
audio_design.score_character: A restrained original ambient bed: low drone, soft field-noise texture, and a small pulse only during build/system sections. No stock cinematic swell.

section_volume_plan.intent: Let the first sentence land with almost no music.
Low pressure under the origin/problem evidence.
Slight lift for privilege, v0, and the ride to Jiri.
Duck for fieldwork and the phone/UX quote so the point is heard.
Steady build while Gemma, intake, RAG, and interviews assemble into a system.
Hold back during the anti-hallucination and officer-loop claim.
Controlled lift for custom ASR/TTS and voice pipeline proof.
Stay under real one-word question cuts while the human grid and final definition land.

global_motion.ambient: grain, subtle parallax on footage, slow push on photos and static cards
global_motion.intentional: hard cuts for evidence, mask reveal for Jiri, assembly animation for systems

rules.text_rule: On-screen text must add information beyond narration, except subtitles and title cards.
rules.asset_rule: Every ready render asset in assets/manifest.json must appear in at least one visible beat layer. Global narration/ASR assets must appear in global_assets. Reference assets must either be visible in a beat or moved out of ready assets.
validation_required: render start/mid/end still for every beat
verify every referenced asset is visible at least once
verify text is readable and not overlapping
verify animation evidence from start to mid frames
rules.layout_non_repetition: No more than two adjacent beats may use the same screen-plus-text-plus-diagram grammar; pattern breaks are required at B07, B10, B16, and B20.
rules.beat_readiness_rule: A beat marked ready must include selected source windows or explicit layer-level asset bindings, not only a vague source clip.

beats.name: Cold Open
beats.purpose: Establish lived public-service pain before any tech appears.
beats.narration_cue: Nepal has a problem.

layers.text: Nepal has a problem.

beats.name: Hidden Knowledge Has A Price
beats.purpose: Turn origin story into concrete numbers and route pain.
beats.narration_cue: Three weeks. Four offices. Four versions of the same form. Almost eight thousand rupees paid to middlemen.

layers.description: Annotated route board from the real company/PAN journey fills the frame and shows office hops, repeated forms, and route failure.
layers.validation: route_map is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.animation: static readable proof board; number chips live in the side gutter so they do not cover the diagram
layers.position: center contain, side-gutter number chips

layers.description: Extracted pieces from the user's annotated route image: online form, company registrar office, IRD offices, and final office.
layers.validation: The route is animated from extracted pieces; the full annotated board is not just used as a static foreground.
layers.animation: cards enter in route order; user marker travels from online form to offices; form-version chips count up

layers.description: 3 weeks, 4 offices, 4 forms, Rs 8k extra remain readable on left.
layers.validation: All four chips readable at 1080p.

layers.description: Side-gutter note says people are paying for information, not the service, while the annotated board carries OCR/CAMIS and IRD Tripureshwor, Kalimati, Kalanki evidence.
layers.validation: source_note is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.position: bottom-right, 20px mono text on paper strip

beats.purpose: Show middlemen/routing problem is broad, not one Reddit post.

beats.name: E-Governance Gap
beats.purpose: Diagnose UX plus documented and undocumented knowledge.
beats.narration_cue: It's the gap every country transitioning into modern e-governance faces. Nepal has internet forms, but you still have to visit offices.

beats.name: Already Learned UX
beats.purpose: Show that the solution must use interfaces Nepalis already understand: voice, call, WhatsApp, kiosk.
beats.narration_cue: The internet of Nepal wasn't built for PDFs, HTML forms, and buttons. People already know how to speak, call, and use WhatsApp. I thought maybe we should start there.

layers.description: Left side shows a confusing government-web experience: portal choices, PDF reading, invalid submission, missing room/counter, and unanswered phone.
layers.validation: The left side clearly communicates a person not knowing how to operate the web/form flow, not merely a neutral website montage.
layers.position: left 48 percent

layers.description: Right side shows WhatsApp as the already-learned UX: voice note, ASR text, follow-up, municipality clarification, answer with sources.
layers.validation: The right side foregrounds WhatsApp usage as the familiar behavior people already know.
layers.position: right 48 percent

layers.description: Hen clip appears at about 01:09:03 in the output timeline, using the pre-trimmed 6s source moment for roughly one second.
layers.validation: The hen appears only during the To Lay / To Not Lay contrast and does not become the whole point.
layers.animation: top-center clipped video, one-second flash at global 69.1s

layers.description: Labels anchor the real point: website UX is not enough; already-learned voice and WhatsApp behavior is the deployment surface.
layers.validation: to_lay_labels is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text:
WEB-FIRST: portals, PDFs, hidden rooms
VOICE-FIRST: ask like a person, route like a system

beats.name: Ask First, Answer Second
beats.purpose: Start the Gemma pivotal proof at the spoken 1:21 cue, clarify the CPT/SFT training story, then switch to the real annotated WhatsApp flow exactly when the narration says the first job is not to answer but understand the case.
beats.narration_cue: Gemma was pivotal because it is small enough for local deployment, but still can reason through messy ASR text, ask the next question, and compose from sources. The first job is not to answer, but to understand the case.

layers.description: Gemma was pivotal title with Raspberry Pi local inference proof and planner/router crop.
layers.validation: The beginning of B12 explains both small-machine deployment and planner/router ability, without stealing the B11 That is the job slot.

layers.description: Architecture crop focused on the planner contract and source router.
layers.validation: Planner contract/resolver and source router are visible beside the Pi proof.

layers.description: Clarify that CPT helped with domain language but SFT trained planner/composer behavior from synthetic Q/A validation and source-context examples.
layers.validation: CPT and SFT are explained in plain terms; the viewer does not mistake the v0-v6 strip for unexplained training data.
layers.text:
CPT: domain language, not enough for routing
SFT: synthetic Q/A validation and source-context planner/composer behavior
Rule: no government fact memorization

layers.description: Real annotated WhatsApp flow: voice note, ASR correction, follow-up question, source-backed answer, and TTS.
layers.validation: At output 1:31 the real WhatsApp flow dominates the frame and the message is understand the case first.

beats.name: RAG Is Necessary But Not Enough
beats.purpose: First show the scattered-truth problem, then show the maintained corpus as infrastructure: DigoBikas seed index, source registry, crawlers, extraction, searchable chunks, messy crawler edge cases, and evidence-path repair.
beats.narration_cue: RAG alone is weak when truth is scattered across old websites, PDFs, office habits, and the memory of one contact officer. So I built a registry of 800+ government sources, crawlers, health checks, and a self-healing pipeline.
beats.transition_out: hold the RAG claim into B14 before interview appears

layers.description: Four evidence fragments appear at the RAG line: old websites, PDF rules, office habits, and contact-officer memory.
layers.validation: At around 1:34 the viewer sees the phrase truth is scattered and four concrete source fragments, not just a generic website background.

layers.description: DigoBikas scraping tmux appears inside the corpus infrastructure board as crawler proof.
layers.validation: crawl_tmux is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: DigoBikas source index flows into registry, crawl, extract, chunks, and source-pack evidence. The hardening snapshot stats are visible: 1,071 sources, 46,051 live documents, 272,718 chunks.
layers.validation: Around 1:40 the viewer sees corpus as infrastructure, the DigoBikas seed, exact hardening counts, and the pipeline from registry to source pack.

layers.description: Broken sites, old PDF encodings, scanned notices, zero-text docs, duplicates, and stale/dead pages appear as the actual work.
layers.validation: Crawler edge cases are visible as concrete failure modes, not hidden behind a generic pipeline label.
layers.text:
broken sites
old PDF encodings
scanned notices
zero-text documents
duplicates
stale / dead pages

layers.description: Self-healing is described as evidence-path repair: missing source -> crawl, stale page -> repair recipe, zero-text PDF -> parse/OCR, weak ranking -> eval/rerank, practical gap -> interview/review.
layers.validation: The repair queue explicitly maps evidence gaps to crawl, parsing, review, or interview work.
layers.text:
missing source -> crawl / discover
dead or stale page -> repair recipe
zero-text PDF -> parse / OCR
duplicate docs -> dedupe index
weak ranking -> eval + rerank
practical gap -> interview / review

layers.description: Answer contract: legal facts from official websites, practical facts from reviewed humans, answer states uncertainty, contact and source support each claim.
layers.validation: The answer contract is visible at the bottom of the infrastructure board.
layers.text:
legal facts: official websites
practical facts: reviewed humans
answer states uncertainty
contact + source supports claim

beats.name: Self-Healing Evidence Repair
beats.purpose: Explain how stale, missing, weak, or tacit evidence becomes repair work instead of hallucinated answers.
beats.narration_cue: Those answers go through review, become practical sources, and can be cited later beside official government sources. Tacit knowledge is only unfair when it stays trapped in one office corridor.

layers.description: Self-healing flowchart: stale/dead sources, zero-chunk pages, tacit room/counter questions, and weak retrieval signals feed a gap detector and repair queue.
layers.validation: At 1:56 the viewer sees a clear flowchart, not the WhatsApp-only image; WhatsApp officer outreach appears only as one repair path.
layers.text: signals -> gap detector -> repair queue -> repair paths -> reviewed evidence returns
layers.position: full-frame flowchart with signals on left, repair queue center, repair paths on right, return loop at bottom
layers.animation: nodes reveal from left to right; flow arrows draw through the repair queue

layers.description: Small source registry receipt anchors the claim that repair returns to the maintained registry.

layers.description: Crawler/tmux receipt anchors crawl and parsing repair work.

beats.name: Human Loop When Source Is Missing
beats.purpose: Show anti-hallucination and WhatsApp officer outreach loop.
beats.narration_cue: When PreVillage does not know, it should not hallucinate. It says what is missing, shows what sources it checked, and turns the citizen's question into an officer outreach message through the WhatsApp bridge. The answer is not invented. It is asked, reviewed, and folded back into the system for the next person.

layers.description: Large self-healing evidence-path board showing the complete missing-source loop: user asks, pipeline states the gap, contact is found from scraped web content, WhatsApp message is sent to the authority, and the reply becomes reviewed evidence.
layers.validation: no_source_flow is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Timed right-side callouts explain why the system does not hallucinate: it says what it checked, exposes the missing fact, routes to the contact authority, and folds the reviewed answer back.
layers.validation: At 2:10 the WhatsApp self-healing flow is readable and the callouts emphasize the gap-to-contact-to-review path.
layers.text: citizen asks -> gap exposed -> contact found -> WhatsApp bridge -> reviewed source

beats.name: Custom Voice Stack
beats.purpose: Show ASR/TTS grunt work and why voice matters.
beats.narration_cue: The internet UX of forms and buttons was not made with Nepali people in mind. Nepal is voice and language rich. So I trained our own TTS and ASR too.

layers.description: Sister voice collection returns as human data/work proof.
layers.validation: voice_collection is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: Kiosk/ASR clip is cropped to the live Nepali transcript area as proof that custom Nepali ASR is part of the stack.
layers.validation: A spoken Nepali input and live transcript are visible; it must not read as generic chat UI.

layers.animation: slide in after sister voice, 8-frame expo out

layers.description: Hugging Face TTS space plays Real Nepali TTS Kala.

layers.validation: tts_training is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

beats.name: Voice Pipeline In Action
beats.purpose: Show the complete speak-to-answer loop as product evidence: speech, ASR, Gemma fixing/planning, retrieval, and TTS response.
beats.narration_cue: A citizen speaks. Our Nepali ASR transcribes. Gemma fixes the rough text and plans the intent. Retrieval finds official and practical sources. Our Nepali TTS speaks back.
overlay_text: speak -> ASR -> Gemma fix -> intent -> retrieve -> TTS

layers.description: Kiosk voice demo shows speech becoming transcript with Pi visible.
layers.validation: kiosk_asr is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.

layers.description: speak -> ASR -> Gemma fix -> intent -> retrieve -> TTS
layers.validation: pipeline_overlay is visible, readable if textual, and supports the beat purpose.
layers.text: speak -> ASR -> Gemma fix -> ask/retrieve -> TTS
layers.position: bottom rail, icons/labels above actual clips, not center text card

layers.description: Chat receipt still proves Gemma asks first before retrieval instead of guessing.
layers.validation: Follow-up question and source-backed answer are visible; this is the Gemma correction/planner/retrieval receipt.

layers.description: TTS Hugging Face Kala clip plays as the spoken answer proof after retrieval.

beats.name: Human Question Grid Build
beats.purpose: Replace the editorial close narration with real people asking government-service questions, one Chirp2-timed word at a time, so the final proof is human demand rather than more narration.
beats.narration_cue: Founder VO has ended; the close is driven by real user question audio cuts.
overlay_text: the interface people already know becomes public-service access
beats.transition_out: grid holds into final title overlay

layers.description: Eleven vertical phone videos fill a 4x3 grid one by one. Each tile starts slightly before the selected word and speaks only the Chirp2-timed word.
layers.validation: By the end of B19 at least ten tiles are visible; no tile shows a filename; each tile has a readable Nepali word chip.
layers.position: 4 columns x 3 rows, centered below the headline
layers.animation: tiles spring in sequentially; audio uses selected word windows from Chirp2 timestamps

layers.description: Chirp2 word-level timestamps define the exact one-word cuts; this is a production reference, not visible text.
layers.validation: Selected words in the visible chips match question_grid_word_beats.tsv.
layers.animation: selected windows: ghar, bikeko, janmadarta, kasari, banaun, sakincha, dharmadevi, nagarpalikama, janmadarta, kasari, sakincha

layers.description: The top line reframes the close as access through familiar interfaces, not a technical slogan.
layers.validation: Headline and subline are readable and do not overlap the grid.
layers.text:
eleven people, eleven questions
the interface people already know becomes public-service access
layers.position: top-left; one short chip at top-right

asset_resolution.semantic_story_clip_paths: footage/selects/story_clips/*.mp4 are semantic aliases used by the spec and manifest.
asset_resolution.remote_note: On the SSD project, create symlinks for semantic aliases or resolve through ALIASES.tsv before rendering. Do not duplicate source footage unnecessarily.
